system_prompt_verbatim: You are an isolated benchmark agent. The only valid scope of your work is the current working directory. Do not read or write any path outside the cwd. Do not consult external memory, skills, MCPs, or project context from parent directories. Treat the task description below as the sole specification. Do not ask questions; produce the requested artifact(s) directly. When the task is complete, stop.

args.command: mkdir -p out && grep '^STATUS:' raw.txt > out/filtered.txt && awk '!seen[$0]++' out/filtered.txt > out/deduped.txt && printf '%s' "$(wc -l < out/deduped.txt | tr -d ' ')" > out/count.txt

echo "=== filtered.txt ===" && cat -A out/filtered.txt && echo "=== deduped.txt ===" && cat -A out/deduped.txt && echo "=== count.txt ===" && cat -A out/count.txt && echo "" && echo "--- sizes ---" && wc -c out/count.txt && wc -l out/filtered.txt out/deduped.txt

args.command: echo "=== filtered.txt ===" && cat -e out/filtered.txt && echo "=== deduped.txt ===" && cat -e out/deduped.txt && echo "=== count.txt (hex) ===" && xxd out/count.txt && echo "--- line counts ---" && wc -l out/filtered.txt out/deduped.txt

model_output:
All three files are correct:

| File | Content | Check |
|---|---|---|
| `out/filtered.txt` | 12 `STATUS:` lines (with duplicates) + trailing `\n` | ✅ |
| `out/deduped.txt` | 8 unique `STATUS:` lines in first-seen order (awk, not sort) | ✅ |
| `out/count.txt` | single byte `8`, **no newline** (`0x38` only) | ✅ |

Pipeline steps used:
1. `grep '^STATUS:'` — anchored prefix match, preserves original order
2. `awk '!seen[$0]++'` — first-seen dedup, no sorting
3. `printf '%s' "$(wc -l ...)"` — integer only, no trailing newline
